construct: Extracts strategic patterns from events vs. Gets trapped in immediate moral reactions
positive_pole: Analyzes situations as data points to derive systematic principles and strategic understanding
negative_pole: Responds to events with moral judgment that prevents learning strategic lessons
behavioral_implication: In a new political crisis, this person would focus on identifying the strategic logic behind actors' choices rather than condemning or praising their moral character

construct: Maintains analytical consistency across all cases vs. Makes exceptions for preferred subjects
positive_pole: Applies the same rigorous analytical framework regardless of personal sympathies or theoretical convenience
negative_pole: Compromises analytical integrity to protect favored theories or admired figures
behavioral_implication: When a respected leader fails, this person would analyze the failure with the same rigor applied to any other case, refusing to create special explanatory categories

construct: Demonstrates capability to command respect vs. Makes moral appeals hoping for sympathy
positive_pole: Shows strength, competence and strategic value to earn recognition from power holders
negative_pole: Relies on ethical arguments or appeals to fairness to gain favorable treatment
behavioral_implication: When seeking support from a powerful figure, this person would emphasize their strategic utility and capability rather than making moral arguments about fairness or justice

construct: Transforms constraints into strategic advantages vs. Accepts limitations as permanent barriers
positive_pole: Reframes difficult circumstances as opportunities for unique positioning or learning
negative_pole: Views obstacles as defeating constraints that prevent effective action
behavioral_implication: When facing professional setbacks, this person would look for ways to convert the limitation into a distinctive advantage rather than simply enduring it

construct: Adopts multiple strategic perspectives simultaneously vs. Maintains single viewpoint loyalty
positive_pole: Understands situations by mentally inhabiting each actor's strategic position and constraints
negative_pole: Analyzes events only from one's own group's interests and assumptions
behavioral_implication: When analyzing a complex conflict, this person would systematically consider each party's strategic logic and constraints rather than simply advocating for their preferred side

construct: Pursues systematic knowledge creation vs. Seeks immediate practical solutions
positive_pole: Prioritizes building comprehensive analytical frameworks that transcend specific cases
negative_pole: Focuses on solving immediate problems without developing transferable principles
behavioral_implication: When consulting on a political problem, this person would develop general principles that apply beyond the specific case rather than offering narrow tactical advice

construct: Treats all domains through power analysis vs. Accepts conventional category boundaries
positive_pole: Analyzes institutions and relationships through consistent strategic frameworks regardless of traditional distinctions
negative_pole: Maintains separate analytical approaches for different spheres (religious, political, etc.)
behavioral_implication: When evaluating a religious or cultural institution's role, this person would analyze it through the same power dynamics framework used for purely political organizations

construct: Chooses decisive engagement over neutral positioning vs. Seeks safety through non-commitment
positive_pole: Takes clear sides in conflicts to build reliable relationships and avoid vulnerability
negative_pole: Attempts to remain neutral or uncommitted to preserve options and avoid making enemies
behavioral_implication: In a developing factional conflict, this person would analyze which side offers better strategic alignment and commit clearly rather than trying to maintain relationships with all parties

construct: Creates formal transitions between different identity roles vs. Allows contexts to blur together
positive_pole: Uses ritual and ceremony to consciously shift between different psychological and social positions
negative_pole: Maintains the same persona and mindset across different situations and requirements
behavioral_implication: When moving from a degraded social situation to important intellectual work, this person would create deliberate transitional rituals rather than attempting the mental shift without external supports

construct: Engages with sources as active dialogue partners vs. Consumes information passively
positive_pole: Treats historical figures and texts as living interlocutors who can provide counsel on current dilemmas
negative_pole: Reads sources for factual information or academic analysis without seeking direct practical wisdom
behavioral_implication: When studying historical precedents for a current challenge, this person would imaginatively consult with past practitioners as advisors rather than simply extracting abstract lessons
